title: Schedule 14: Protected Persons and Convention Refugees (IMM 0008 SCH14) - Canada.ca
content:
## Schedule 14: Protected Persons and Convention Refugees (IMM 0008 SCH14)
Schedule 14: Protected Persons and Convention Refugees [IMM 0008 SCH14] (PDF,
                    1.8 MB)

### How to download, save and open this form
- Use your computer. The form may not open on tablets or mobile phones .
- Save the form on your computer in a place you can remember. For most Internet browsers, clicking on the link above will automatically download the form. If the form doesn’t automatically download, right-click on the link and select “Save as.”
- For most Internet browsers, clicking on the link above will automatically download the form.
- If the form doesn’t automatically download, right-click on the link and select “Save as.”
- After you download the form, open it using Adobe Acrobat Reader.

#### Open this form in Acrobat Reader
- Open Acrobat Reader. You need Acrobat Reader version 10 or higher to open our forms. Get the latest version of Acrobat Reader .
- You need Acrobat Reader version 10 or higher to open our forms.
- Get the latest version of Acrobat Reader .
- Select “ File ” from the top menu.
- Find the location where you saved the form, click on the file, and click “ Open. ”
Read the step by step instructions on how to complete the form.

### Schedule 14: Protected Persons and Convention Refugees
(IMM 0008–Schedule 14)

#### Who needs to fill out this form?
This form must be completed by:
- you, the principal applicant;
- any person included in the application for permanent residence who has also been found to be a protected person including your spouse or common-law partner, and all dependent children age 18 years or older.
Indicate your full last name (surname/family name) as it appears on your passport or on the official documents that you will use to obtain your passport.
Indicate all of your given names (first, second or more) as they appear on your passport or official documents. Do not use initials.
Indicate your date of birth.
Indicate your Client ID/UCI.
- Note : This is your Client Identification Number (Client ID) also referred to as a Universal Client Identification Number (UCI). You can find your Client ID (8 or 10-digit number) on any official document issued by an IRCC Office.
Check one box to indicate if you have ever been granted permanent residence in Canada.
Note : A permanent resident is someone who has acquired permanent resident status by immigrating to Canada, but is not yet a Canadian citizen.
- Check one box to indicate whether you were recognized as a Convention refugee by a country other than Canada.
- If you chose ‘Yes’, check one box to indicate whether you can return to that country.
- Check one box to indicate if you are a citizen or national of more than one country.
- If you chose ‘Yes’, indicate what countries. Do not use abbreviations.
- Check one box to indicate whether you are a legal and permanent resident of any country.
- If you chose ‘Yes’, indicate what countries. Do not use abbreviations.
- If you chose ‘Yes’ in 7(a), check one box to indicate whether you can return to any of the countries listed in 7(b).
You must sign and date this application form.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
